    <h1 {...{
      "id": "reporting-api-overview"
    }}>{`Reporting API overview`}</h1>
    <p>{`The `}<strong parentName="p">{`Report`}</strong>{` endpoint is the core mechanism for retrieving data from Adobe Analytics 2.0 APIs. It enables you to generate fully customized reports by defining metrics, dimensions, date ranges, filters, and breakdowns in a single request body. Use the Report endpoint when you need:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Custom metric and dimension combinations`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Time-series analysis`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Filtered and segmented reporting`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Nested breakdowns`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Anomaly analysis`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Realtime reporting`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Programmatic report generation for dashboards or automation`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`The Report endpoint is designed for flexibility and scalability, supporting both simple KPI queries and advanced analytical workflows.`}</p>
    <p>{`For instructions on building specific types of API reports, see the following guides:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/analytics-apis/docs/2.0/guides/endpoints/reports/real-time/"
          }}><strong parentName="a">{`Reporting API first calls`}</strong></a>{`: Get a top items report and create a new report for pageviews, visits, and vistors.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/analytics-apis/docs/2.0/guides/endpoints/reports/anomaly/"
          }}><strong parentName="a">{`Anomaly detection report`}</strong></a>{`: Identify statistically significant deviations in your data using anomaly detection.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/analytics-apis/docs/2.0/guides/endpoints/reports/breakdowns/"
          }}><strong parentName="a">{`Breakdown reports`}</strong></a>{`: Generate multi-level reports by breaking down dimension items into additional dimensions.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/analytics-apis/docs/2.0/guides/endpoints/reports/kpi/"
          }}><strong parentName="a">{`Date-trended basic report (KPI report)`}</strong></a>{`: Create date-trended reports to track key performance indicators over time.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/analytics-apis/docs/2.0/guides/endpoints/reports/advanced/"
          }}><strong parentName="a">{`Date-trended advanced report`}</strong></a>{`: Create advanced date-trended API reports using date range comparisons and segments.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><a parentName="p" {...{
            "href": "debugger.md"
          }}><strong parentName="a">{`Debugger for Analysis Workspace`}</strong></a>{`: Use Oberon in Analysis Workspace to see XML and API calls with JSON debugger.`}</p>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">

      </li>

      </li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "creating-api-reports"
    }}>{`Creating API Reports`}</h2>
    <p>{`Most Adobe Analytics reports are generated using the `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`POST`}</inlineCode></strong>{` method. This design allows you to submit a structured JSON payload that defines:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The report suite (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`rsid`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Global and metric-specific filters`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Dimensions and metrics`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Date ranges`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Sorting and pagination settings`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Breakdowns and nested queries`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Using `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`POST`}</inlineCode>{` provides the flexibility required for complex analytical queries, including multi-level breakdowns, anomaly detection, and advanced filtering.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "exception-get-top-items-report"
    }}>{`Exception: GET top items report`}</h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://developer.adobe.com/analytics-apis/docs/2.0/apis/#operation/runTopItemReport"
        }}><strong parentName="a">{`GET top items report`}</strong></a></li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This endpoint uses the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`GET`}</inlineCode>{` method and is designed specifically for retrieving top dimension items in a simplified format. It does not support the full report definition structure available through `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`POST /reports`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`Unless you are using the Top Items report, all report generation should use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`POST`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "report-apis-provide-data-structures-not-visualizations"
    }}>{`Report APIs provide data structures, not visualizations`}</h2>
    <p>{`When you design reports via the Report API, you interact with the data foundation used by Analysis Workspace or another intelligence tool. API structures do not specify the presentation layers built on top of the data. You will not see a visualization parameter type in the JSON request or response.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "using-dimension-in-report-payload-requests"
    }}>{`Using `}<inlineCode parentName="h2">{`dimension`}</inlineCode>{` in report payload requests`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dimension`}</inlineCode>{` object member is not required in report request payloads. When no dimension is provided:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The request is effectively a `}<strong parentName="li">{`Totals`}</strong>{` report.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The response contains only `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`summaryData`}</inlineCode>{` with totals for the requested metrics over the specified date range.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`rows`}</inlineCode>{` array will be absent or empty. No dimension breakdown will be provided in the respone body.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "example-totals-report-no-dimension"
    }}>{`Example: Totals report (no dimension)`}</h3>
    <p>{`To retrieve only `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`metric`}</inlineCode>{` totals for a date range, omit the dimension property from the report request. The response will contain `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`summaryData`}</inlineCode>{` totals only and no rows.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "date-range-field--supported-formats"
    }}>{`Date-range field – supported formats`}</h2>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dateRange`}</inlineCode>{` field in `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`globalFilters`}</inlineCode>{` supports three formats:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Absolute date range`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Date formula`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Mixed`}</li>
    </ol>
    <p>{`The following sections describe each format.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "absolute-date-range"
    }}>{`Absolute date range`}</h3>
    <p>{`In this format, the start and end date are specified in ISO 8601 format, separated by `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/`}</inlineCode>{`. This format is supported for all date ranges, including metric level filters outside of a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`globalFilters`}</inlineCode>{` array of objects.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Format:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`<start_date>/<end_date>
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Examples:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`2024-01-01T00:00:00.000/2024-02-01T00:00:00.000
2024-01-01T00:00/2024-02-01T00:00
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "date-formula"
    }}>{`Date formula`}</h3>
    <p>{`This format includes a dynamic date range in a formula syntax. Both the start and end are expressed as formulas, separated by `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/`}</inlineCode>{`. Formulas are evaluated relative to the report suite timezone (Analytics) or Data View timezone (Customer Journey Analytics). This format is only supported for fields within a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`globalFilters`}</inlineCode>{` array of objects and is only available for ranked reports--not realtime reports.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Format:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`<start_formula>/<end_formula>
`}</code></pre>
    <h4 {...{

    </table>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "shift-modifiers"
    }}>{`Shift Modifiers`}</h4>
    <p>{`Append `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`+`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`-`}</inlineCode>{` followed by a number and a unit to shift the base, according to the following table:`}</p>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Modifier`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Meaning`}</th>
        </tr>
      </thead>
      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`+/-Nh`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Hours`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`+/-Nd`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Days`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`+/-Nw`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Weeks`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`+/-Nm`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Months`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`+/-Nq`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}>{`Quarters`}</td>
        </tr>
        <tr parentName="tbody">
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null
          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`+/-Ny`}</inlineCode></td>
          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null

    </table>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "examples"
    }}>{`Examples`}</h3>
    <table>
      <thead parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="thead">
          <th parentName="tr" {...{

          }}>{`Formula`}</th>
          <th parentName="tr" {...{

      <tbody parentName="table">
        <tr parentName="tbody">

          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`th-24h/th`}</inlineCode></td>

        <tr parentName="tbody">

          }}><inlineCode parentName="td">{`ty-1y/ty`}</inlineCode></td>

        <tr parentName="tbody">

        <tr parentName="tbody">

          <td parentName="tr" {...{
            "align": null

        </tr>
      </tbody>
    </table>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Example description`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`td-7d/td`}</inlineCode>{` means "7 days ago to today."`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "mixed-format"
    }}>{`Mixed format`}</h3>
    <p>{`This format combines an absolute ISO 8601 date on one side with a formula on the other, separated by `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`/`}</inlineCode>{`. The formula side is evaluated dynamically; the absolute side is a fixed date. This format is only supported for fields within a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`globalFilters`}</inlineCode>{` array of objects and is only available for ranked reports--not realtime reports.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Examples:`}</strong></p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`2024-01-01T00:00:00/th       (From Jan 1, 2024 to the current hour)
tm-1m/2024-06-01T00:00:00   (From the start of last month to June 1, 2024)
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The syntax for this format is strict. Use the exact formula codes and ISO 8601 date format as shown. Do not use angle brackets, quotes, or other escaping characters around formula values.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "timezone-behavior-in-date-ranges"
    }}>{`Timezone behavior in date ranges`}</h3>
    <p>{`Date ranges with formulas are evaluated in the configured timezone of the data source:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Adobe Analytics:`}</strong>{` report suite timezone`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`CJA:`}</strong>{` Data View timezone`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`If no timezone is configured, the API returns a 400 status code error, such as:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-text"
      }}>{`Could not determine timezone for dataId=<dataId>
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "date-ranges-for-realtime-reports"
    }}>{`Date ranges for realtime reports`}</h3>
    <p>{`The date formula and mixed formats are not available for realtime reports. For realtime reports, use the existing `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`dateRangeRelative`}</inlineCode>{` field.`}</p>
